title: Assertion fault on DISTINCT query containing a correlated aggregate.
description:
drh added on 2015-11-24 02:08:45:
The following SQL causes an ALWAYS() macro to fail:
SELECT (SELECT 'mmm' UNION SELECT DISTINCT max(name) ORDER BY 1) 
  FROM sqlite_master;
This problem appears to have been introduced by check-in [7ab0b258eabfcfb7]
on 2015-09-05 for release 3.9.0.  The problem was discovered by 
Kostya Serebryany using libFuzzer.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value DOUBLE PRECISION, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Alice', 10.5), ('Bob', 20.0), ('Charlie', NULL), ('David', 15.0);
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INTEGER, description VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT (SELECT 'mmm' UNION SELECT DISTINCT max(name) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) FROM sys.tables;
key_bug_features: DISTINCT query with correlated aggregate causes assertion fault
